rationale_for_baseline_tier: A new temporal headache with constitutional symptoms and a raised ESR in a >50-year-old is a high pre-test probability for sight-threatening GCA that telehealth cannot examine or blood-test. Urgent same-day in-person assessment (T3) for ESR/CRP, temporal artery examination and steroid consideration is the correct baseline — escalating immediately to T4 (ED) if any visual symptom or jaw claudication appears. Overnight reassurance/routine follow-up is unsafe.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Any visual change (blurring, transient obscuration, double vision, visual loss) or new jaw claudication/scalp tenderness
escalation_edges.rationale: Signals impending/actual ischaemic visual loss from GCA needing emergency high-dose steroids.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If you notice any change in your vision, or your jaw aches when chewing, go to the emergency department immediately — this can threaten your sight and needs urgent treatment.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Cannot obtain same-day assessment, or headache/constitutional symptoms worsen
escalation_edges.rationale: Ensures the same-day GCA work-up/steroid window is not lost.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If you can't be seen today, go to the emergency department so the blood tests and examination can be done without delay.

contextual_modifiers.patient_factor: Older adult (>50) with new temporal headache
contextual_modifiers.effect_on_advice: Age markedly raises GCA probability; do not defer assessment.

baseline_safety_net_advice.default_tier_advice: Because of your age, the new one-sided temple headache, feeling run-down, and the raised inflammation marker, this needs checking in person TODAY — it could be an artery inflammation that can affect sight if untreated. Use paracetamol meanwhile. Go straight to the emergency department if you get ANY change in vision or jaw pain when chewing.
baseline_safety_net_advice.contact_method: Urgent same-day in-person assessment (GP/rheumatology); ED for visual symptoms
baseline_safety_net_advice.after_hours_advice: If you cannot be seen same-day, attend the emergency department rather than waiting.
baseline_safety_net_advice.documentation_for_gp: Telehealth review of new temporal headache + constitutional symptoms + raised ESR in a 72-year-old; GCA cannot be excluded remotely; urgent same-day ESR/CRP, temporal artery exam and steroid consideration advised; visual-symptom emergency safety-net given.

critical_under_triage.failure_description: Labelling as tension headache and advising routine review, losing the same-day GCA window.
critical_under_triage.patient_harm_scenario: An untreated GCA progresses to sudden anterior ischaemic optic neuropathy overnight, causing permanent, potentially bilateral blindness.

serious_under_triage.failure_description: Prompt (24-48 h) review is too slow for suspected GCA; same-day (T3) is required.

moderate_over_triage.failure_description: Ambulance is unnecessary without visual symptoms; same-day in-person assessment (T3) or ED (T4) is appropriate.

clinical_uncertainty_handling.key_uncertainty: Cannot examine temporal arteries, perform fundoscopy, or obtain ESR/CRP remotely to exclude GCA.
clinical_uncertainty_handling.correct_response_to_uncertainty: Treat as possible GCA: urgent same-day in-person assessment and steroid consideration, with an emergency visual-symptom safety-net.
clinical_uncertainty_handling.wrong_response_to_uncertainty: Attribute to tension headache and reassure overnight.
